QF.id: VI Functions of DP Account Operations_9

question.text: NSDL's SPEED-e facility, launched in September 2001, primarily enables demat account holders to:

answer.text: Submit delivery instructions directly on the Internet

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SPEED-e enables demat account holders to submit delivery instructions directly on the Internet through the SPEED-e website. **The Trap:** 'Trade on stock exchanges' is done through trading platforms, not SPEED-e. SPEED-e handles the demat delivery side, not the trading side. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VII covers the delivery instruction mechanism in trade settlement. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Password users can debit only to pre-notified CM accounts (up to 6). Smart Card/e-Token users can submit instructions to any account — more flexibility but requires stronger authentication.
